# mermaid-preview

> Lightweight CLI to preview Mermaid diagrams in the browser.

## Agent Usage (recommended)

Pipe mermaid source to stdin. The CLI opens a browser window and
exits immediately with code 0. No server process is left running.

    echo "graph TD; A-->B-->C" | mermaid-preview

The browser window is self-contained — it works after the CLI exits.

## File Preview (with live reload)

    mermaid-preview diagram.mmd
    mermaid-preview flow.mmd sequence.mmd
    mermaid-preview README.md

File mode starts a local server with live reload. The CLI stays running.

## Flags

    -p, --port PORT       Server port (default: auto)
    -b, --no-browser      Don't auto-open browser
    -t, --theme THEME     dark | light | system (default: system)
    -w, --no-watch        Disable file watching
        --poll INTERVAL   Polling fallback (e.g. 500ms)
        --once            Render to self-contained HTML and exit
                          (default for stdin, optional for files)
        --serve           Force server mode (override --once for stdin)
    -v, --version         Print version
    -h, --help            Print help

## Supported Files

    .mmd, .mermaid        Mermaid diagram files
    .md, .markdown        Extracts ```mermaid fenced blocks

## Stdin

    Default behavior: renders to temp HTML file and exits (--once).
    Use --serve to keep a server running instead.
    Max input: 10MB.

## Exit Codes

    0  success
    1  argument error
    2  runtime error

## Stderr

    All output: "mermaid-preview: <message>"
    Once mode:  "mermaid-preview: wrote /tmp/mermaid-preview-XXXX.html"
    Server mode: "mermaid-preview: listening on http://127.0.0.1:PORT"
